title: how to run portainer for podman in wsl ?
body:
Hi
First of all
I know this is a [duplicate](https://github.com/portainer/portainer/issues/2991) But I could not perform in the end.
I want to run portainer in windows linux subsystem [ wsl 2 ]
I runthe following code
`podman system service --time 0`
Then the following code
`podman run -d -p 8000: 8000 -p 9000: 9000 --name = portainer --restart = always -v /run/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock -v portainer_data: / data portainer / portainer-ce
`
But it does not work and portainer cant connect to podman 
Can anyone guide me?
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28746285/113750078-9553a780-971f-11eb-9fdf-6327ba9e21d3.png)

comments.body: Can you try `--time 0`? Using a timeout without systemd socket activation doesn't make sense.

> Can you try `--time 0`? Using a timeout without systemd socket activation doesn't make sense.

yes i try but not work...
for u work ?

@id1385 I succeeded to run Podman on Fedora34 distro with enabled via systemd-genie systemd as cgroup manager but the networking is the real mess. In my Insider preview and hand-made Fedora34 the entire networking looks like pure IPV6 and even NGINX writes "listening for ipv6 by default ...".  NGINX responds inside the distro but I can't test it via browser. The wslconfig configuration option localhost forwarding meaning is not clearly documented. In which stack it works, IPV4 or IPV6?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am not sure how this is an upstream issue, I am closing, if people are still interested and there is something we can fix, then reopen it.
